records.ety: [L. pronus, akin to Gr. <?/, <?/, Skr. pravana sloping, inclined, and also to L. pro forward, for. See Pro-.]

quotes: Towards him they bend With awful reverence prone.

defs: Prostrate; flat; esp., lying with the face down; -- opposed to supine.
quotes: Which, as the wind, Blew where it listed, laying all things prone.

defs: Sloping, with reference to a line or surface; declivous; inclined; not level.
quotes: Since the floods demand, For their descent, a prone and sinking land.

defs: Inclined; propense; disposed; -- applied to the mind or affections, usually in an ill sense. Followed by to.
quotes: Poets are nearly all prone to melancholy.
